title: Governor Ferguson Condemns Trump AdministrationГўв‚¬в„ўs Termination of $1.1B in Energy Grant Funding in Hydrogen Hub
content:
On October 2, 2025, Governor Bob Ferguson strongly criticized the Trump AdministrationГўв‚¬в„ўs decision to terminate more than $1.1 billion in federal funding for energy projects across Washington State. The most significant portion of the cuts includes $1 billion allocated to the Pacific Northwest Hydrogen Hub, a key initiative aimed at developing a clean hydrogen economy, creating over 10,000 jobs, and reducing emissions from hard-to-decarbonize sectors such as industry and transportation.

The funding cuts are part of a broader $7.5 billion rollback by the U.S. Department of Energy, affecting several states Гўв‚¬вЂќ many of them Democratic-led Гўв‚¬вЂќ and come amid a federal government shutdown impacting approximately 80,000 federal workers in Washington. Governor Ferguson condemned the move as a politically motivated and unlawful action, emphasizing that it jeopardizes job creation, economic growth, and climate progress. He announced collaboration with the Washington Attorney GeneralГўв‚¬в„ўs Office to challenge the decision legally.

The terminated funds were tied to 11 clean energy projects critical to WashingtonГўв‚¬в„ўs progress toward net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050. These initiatives included efforts to expand zero-emission commercial vehicles, enhance solar panel efficiency, develop clean hydrogen infrastructure, and support industrial decarbonization. The affected projects span at least five counties, with major recipients including the Pacific Northwest Hydrogen Association ($1B), PACCAR Inc. ($68M), Nippon Dynawave ($46.6M), CleanFiber Inc. ($10M), Spokane Edo LLC ($5.3M), SilFab Solar WA Inc. ($3M), and Washington State University ($9.8M) for multiple research-based clean energy projects.

Governor Ferguson reaffirmed his administrationГўв‚¬в„ўs commitment to protecting WashingtonГўв‚¬в„ўs clean energy future, maintaining that these investments are essential for innovation, affordability, and environmental resilience in the stateГўв‚¬в„ўs long-term climate strategy.
author: Analysts Account
